# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder_6.7b-instruct)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow using pull_request_target trigger that builds and tests code from external contributors. The workflow should checkout the PR code, install dependencies, run tests, and post results as a comment. This enables automated testing of community contributions.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Test Pull Request
on: 
  pull_request_target:
    types: [opened, synchronize, reopened]
jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v2
        with:
          token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          repository: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.repo.full_name }}
      
      - name: Setup Node.js environment
        uses: actions/setup-node@v2
        with:
          node-version: '14'
          
      - name: Install Dependencies
        run: npm ci
        
      - name: Run Tests
        run: npm test
        
      - name: Post Test Results as Comment
        uses: actions/github-script@v3
        with:
          github-token: ${{secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN}}
          script: |
            const fs = require('fs');
            const {pullNumber, repo} = context;
            let results = fs.readFileSync('/home/runner/work/_temp/_github_home/nodejs-pr/npm-test-results.xml', 'utf8');
            console.log("Pull request number is " + pullNumber); 
            console.log(repo)
            const octokit = github.getOctokit(process.env["GITHUB_TOKEN"]);
            octokit.issues.createComment({
              owner: context.repo.owner,
              repo: repo.name,
              issue_number: pullNumber,
              body: `Test results:\n\`\`\`${results}\`\`\``
            })
